BUSINESS CASE: AI-Powered Diagnostic Platform — OncoDetect Pro

PRODUCT DESCRIPTION
OncoDetect Pro is a machine learning-based liquid biopsy platform for multi-cancer early detection (MCED). It analyzes circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) methylation patterns to screen for 12 cancer types simultaneously from a single blood draw.

CLINICAL EVIDENCE
- DETECT-1 Pivotal Trial (N=8,400): Sensitivity 67.3% across all stages; specificity 99.1%
- Stage I-II sensitivity: 43.9% (the key challenge — many cancers detected too late)
- Cancer Signal Origin (CSO) accuracy: 89% for correctly identifying tissue of origin
- False positive rate: 0.9% — low but concerning at population screening scale

REGULATORY STATUS
- FDA Breakthrough Device Designation granted
- De novo 510(k) pathway — no predicate device; regulatory timeline uncertain
- CMS parallel review initiated for coverage determination
- EU MDR classification pending

MARKET DYNAMICS
- Total addressable market: $50B globally for cancer screening
- Competitor: Grail's Galleri test (first-mover, backed by Illumina, $949/test)
- Pricing strategy: Targeting $799/test, below Galleri but above standard screenings
- Reimbursement: No CPT code yet; out-of-pocket for most patients currently
- Provider adoption: Primary care physicians unfamiliar with liquid biopsy interpretation
- Patient demand: Growing consumer awareness of multi-cancer screening via DTC marketing

KEY UNCERTAINTIES
1. Will FDA require a randomized controlled trial showing mortality benefit?
2. Can CMS coverage be secured without long-term outcomes data?
3. How quickly will PCPs adopt vs specialist-driven ordering?
4. What happens when Grail drops pricing in response?
